original_hypothesis.title: PSM decimalCorrection may underflow / revert for peg tokens with >18 decimals, blocking PSM deployment or bricking mint/redeem paths
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: SimplePSM computes `decimalCorrection = 10 ** (18 - decimals)` using `ERC20(_pegToken).decimals()`. If the configured peg token reports decimals > 18 (or a malicious token returns an unexpected value), the subtraction underflows and deployment reverts, or the PSM becomes unusable. This is a concrete code defect: no bounds check on decimals.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Even if intended peg tokens are 6/18 decimals, a misconfigured deployment (or token upgrade changing decimals semantics) can permanently prevent minting/redemption, breaking the peg mechanism and impacting `ProfitManager.totalBorrowedCredit()` assumptions.
deployment_assumptions: The deployed pegToken has decimals() > 18 OR can change behavior post-deploy (upgradeable token) to report >18
Protocol relies on SimplePSM mint/redeem for peg maintenance
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment script wiring/validation is provided for SimplePSM pegToken decimals bounds; SimplePSM constructor trusts ERC20(_pegToken).decimals() without range checks.

proof_of_signal.pattern: decimalCorrection = 10 ** (18 - decimals); subtraction underflows if decimals>18

preconditions: SimplePSM is deployed/configured with a pegToken whose decimals() returns > 18 (or changes to >18)
No external wrapper enforces pegToken decimals <= 18

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: unchecked assumption about ERC20.decimals() range leading to underflow in exponent base
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: PSM deployment or operation can revert entirely for misconfigured/malicious peg token

analysis_questions: Is pegToken guaranteed by governance process to have decimals <= 18 on all target chains?
Is pegToken upgradeable (can decimals() behavior change)?
Is there any deployment-time validation elsewhere for pegToken decimals bounds?
validation_requirements: Unit test deploying SimplePSM with decimals 19/24 to confirm revert
Review governance/onboarding procedures for pegToken selection/verification

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker (or misconfiguration) causes deployment/configuration of a PSM with a token reporting >18 decimals; PSM cannot be deployed or becomes non-functional, breaking mint/redeem utility and potentially destabilizing the CREDIT peg.
prerequisites: Governance/config error OR pegToken is upgradeable/malicious

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Peg mechanism unavailable; could materially impact protocol stability and user redemptions
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All CREDIT holders relying on PSM liquidity
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires redeploy/migrate PSM or change peg token

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No evidence of tests ensuring decimals <= 18 or safe scaling behavior

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Check deployed pegToken decimals and upgradeability

validation_result.impact_summary: PSM deployment reverts for peg tokens with >18 decimals due to unhandled arithmetic underflow.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The `SimplePSM` constructor retrieves the `decimals` of the configured `_pegToken` and calculates a `decimalCorrection` multiplier using `10 ** (18 - decimals)`. If the provided token has strictly more than 18 decimals (e.g., 24 decimals like YAM-V2), the subtraction `18 - decimals` will trigger an arithmetic underflow panic in Solidity 0.8.x. This causes the deployment transaction to revert entirely. While this fail-safe prevents the contract from operating with incorrect scaling, it is an unhandled edge case that restricts the protocol from supporting tokens with >18 decimals. To support such tokens, the contract would need conditional logic to divide by `10 ** (decimals - 18)` instead of multiplying.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The provided proof of signal accurately points to the constructor of `SimplePSM.sol`, though the exact line numbers in the provided source are 71-72. The code shows the unchecked subtraction `18 - decimals` which will underflow if `decimals > 18`.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The subtraction `18 - decimals` assumes that the token's decimals will always be less than or equal to 18. If a token with >18 decimals is used, this reverts with an arithmetic underflow.

execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: When `SimplePSM` is deployed, the constructor reads `ERC20(_pegToken).decimals()`. If the returned value is greater than 18, the expression `18 - decimals` underflows, reverting the deployment.

deployment_assumptions_checked: The deployed pegToken has decimals() > 18 OR can change behavior post-deploy (upgradeable token) to report >18
Protocol relies on SimplePSM mint/redeem for peg maintenance
config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided specify the exact peg token to be used or its decimals. Therefore, it cannot be verified whether a token with >18 decimals will be used.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: If governance attempts to deploy a PSM for a stablecoin with >18 decimals, the deployment will revert. This acts as a Denial of Service at configuration time, preventing the protocol from expanding to such assets without modifying the smart contract.
validation_result.impact_assessment: The impact is limited to configuration-time Denial of Service. No user funds are at risk, but the protocol cannot support tokens with >18 decimals.
recommended_mitigations: Implement conditional logic to handle tokens with >18 decimals by dividing by `10 ** (decimals - 18)` during mint/redeem calculations, or add an explicit `require(decimals <= 18, "Unsupported decimals");` to provide a clear error message instead of an underflow panic.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a mock ERC20 token that returns 24 for `decimals()`. 2. Attempt to deploy `SimplePSM` passing the mock token as `_pegToken`. 3. Observe the transaction reverting with a Panic(0x11) arithmetic underflow.
uncertainties: It is unclear if the protocol ever intends to support peg tokens with >18 decimals.
additional_testing_needed: Test PSM deployment with tokens having exactly 18 decimals, <18 decimals, and >18 decimals to ensure expected behavior across all ranges.
